description: Chemistry fundamentals - reactions, electrochemistry, materials science.

facts.context: SN1 vs SN2 nucleophilic substitution reactions differ in:
distractors: only the leaving group that is involved in the mechanism
temperature requirements for the reaction to proceed
whether a catalyst is required for completion

facts.context: A galvanic cell reaction is spontaneous when:
distractors: cell potential E is negative indicating energy release
electrode potentials are exactly equal to each other
external voltage is applied to drive the reaction

facts.truth: d-orbital splitting in transition metal complexes
distractors: covalent bond formation between atoms in molecules
ionic compound solubility in polar solvents only
metallic conductivity in pure metal samples

facts.context: Below the glass transition temperature Tg, a polymer is:
distractors: rubbery and flexible allowing large deformations
completely crystalline with ordered structure
liquid with high molecular mobility
p-type semiconductor with mobile holes as carriers
an insulator with no free charge carriers at all
a metallic conductor with delocalized electrons

facts.context: Markovnikov's rule for HX addition to alkenes states:
facts.truth: H adds to carbon with more hydrogens
distractors: X always adds to the more substituted carbon atom
addition always proceeds through anti-Markovnikov pathway
the reaction requires a peroxide catalyst present

facts.context: Le Chatelier's principle states that equilibrium shifts to:
facts.truth: counteract applied stress on the system
distractors: always favor product formation regardless of conditions
increase the total pressure of the system always
maximize entropy change in the forward direction

facts.context: Hess's Law states that enthalpy change:
distractors: depends on the specific reaction pathway taken
is always negative for spontaneous reactions only
equals the activation energy of the reaction

facts.context: In a redox reaction, the oxidizing agent:
distractors: loses electrons and becomes more positive charge
remains unchanged throughout the entire reaction
always contains oxygen atoms in its structure

facts.context: A buffer solution resists pH changes because:
facts.truth: weak acid/base pair neutralizes added H+ or OH-
distractors: strong acids prevent any pH change whatsoever
the solution contains no ionizable species at all
water's autoionization is completely suppressed

facts.context: Strong field ligands in transition metal complexes cause:
facts.truth: large d-orbital splitting favoring low spin
distractors: small d-orbital splitting favoring high spin states
no splitting of d-orbitals at all in any case
oxidation of the central metal ion always

facts.context: The band gap in semiconductors determines:
facts.truth: energy needed to excite electrons to conduction band
distractors: the physical distance between atoms in the lattice
melting point of the semiconductor material only
magnetic properties of the material exclusively
